---
title: Overview
aliases:
  - ../index.html
---

**tspaint** infers soft, calibrated **local ancestry** along individual haplotypes from an
inferred **tree sequence** (Relate → tskit, or tsinfer/SINGER). Ancestry is modelled as a
discrete character evolving up the branches of each marginal tree under a continuous-time
Markov chain (a CTMC "mugration" model); reference haplotypes label tips through a soft noise
model with **learned per-tip credibility**; and the parameters are fit by **EM** whose
sufficient statistics are accumulated **per tree-sequence edge, span-weighted** — so a clade
persisting across many trees is counted once. The output is, for every haplotype at every
position, a calibrated posterior `P(ancestry)` — soft, not a hard call.

A separate, optional path — [admixture dating](dating.qmd) — reuses the same engine with a
*time-inhomogeneous* generator to estimate **when** the labelled ancestries diverged or exchanged
genes, as directional cross-ancestry rates `q_AB(t)`, `q_BA(t)` through time. It is its own
deliverable and does not change the painting.

See [the model](model.qmd) for the generative construction, [inference](inference.qmd) for the
EM math, [admixture dating](dating.qmd) for the rate-through-time estimator, and
[assumptions](assumptions.qmd) for the sharp edges.

## Install

```bash
pixi install          # dev environment (tskit, msprime, numpy, scipy, …)
pixi run test         # run the test suite
```

## Quickstart

```python
import tspaint

# 1. Get a tree sequence — simulate admixture with known truth,
#    or build one from genotypes: tspaint.io.infer_tree_sequence / singer_tree_sequences.
ts = tspaint.simulate_admixture(n_admix=10, n_ref=10, sequence_length=2e6,
                              T_admix=100, T_split=5000, Ne=1000, random_seed=1)

# 2. Label the reference haplotypes (sample-node id -> ancestry state); paint the rest.
labels = {...}                              # source-A tips -> 0, source-B tips -> 1
painting = tspaint.paint(ts, labels)          # EM-fit on references, paint the queries

painting.posteriors[q]                      # soft per-position posterior (Segments)
painting.segments(deadband=0.4)             # hard ancestry tracts (tract-length distribution)

# 3. Optional: admixture dating — a separate deliverable, reusing the painting's fit.
rtt = painting.rate_through_time()          # or tspaint.fit_rate_through_time(ts, labels)
rtt.plot()                                  # q_AB(t), q_BA(t): when the ancestries diverged / mixed
```

On an inferred (tsinfer / Relate) ARG, add `smooth=True` to suppress tree-inference-induced
spurious switches with the horizontal BP smoother.

Two routes lead to *admixture timing*: the **tract-length distribution** from
`painting.segments(...)` (an indirect, downstream input to pulse-dating methods), and the
**direct** rate-through-time estimator [`fit_rate_through_time`](dating.qmd), which fits the
cross-ancestry rate as a function of time.

## The deliverable

For each query haplotype, the soft posterior over ancestry at every position (red = ancestry
A, blue = B, white where the local tree cannot tell), with the true ancestry shown beneath
each row:

{{< embed ./notebooks/showcase.ipynb#fig-painting >}}

Collapsed to hard ancestry tracts with a confidence deadband (for tract-length / admixture
dating).

## Where to go next

| Page | What |
|---|---|
| [The model](model.qmd) | Ancestry CTMC, soft emission + credibility, the two Markov structures |
| [Inference](inference.qmd) | Felsenstein pruning, Van Loan rewards, edge-blocked EM |
| [Admixture dating](dating.qmd) | Time-inhomogeneous Q(t): directional cross-ancestry rate through time |
| [Assumptions & limitations](assumptions.qmd) | Identifiability, π-degeneracy, calibration, mugration |
| [Code paths](code_paths.qmd) | Module map and the `paint` pipeline |
| [Results](results.qmd) | Accuracy, calibration, fragmentation, head-to-head, the BP smoother |
| [API reference](../api/) | Every public function and class |
